mappings.rationale: Directly covers Australian history since 1920 including the Great Depression, WWII, postwar prosperity, Aboriginal rights, and immigration policies discussed in subsection B.
Relevant to Australia and New Zealand's continuing Commonwealth relationships, the League of Nations mandate system, and the independence of Papua New Guinea.
Relevant to the decolonization process in Oceania including independence of Papua New Guinea (1975), the dissolution of Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, and independent island states.
Relevant to the Pacific theater of WWII including the rise and fall of Japanese power and its effects on the region discussed in subsection A.2.
Relevant to Australia and New Zealand's participation in regional security alliances and military involvement in the Korean and Vietnam Wars discussed in subsection B.3.
Relevant to the large-scale European and Asian immigration to Australia and the demographic changes it caused discussed in subsection B.4.
Relevant to Australian and New Zealand democratic politics including the constitutional crisis and dismissal of Whitlam (1975) discussed in subsection B.2.
Relevant to the social welfare programs in Australia under Labor governments and New Zealand's Labour Party welfare programs discussed in subsections B.2 and C.1.
Relevant to feminism and the women's movement in Australian social developments discussed in subsection B.4.
Relevant to Australia's opposition to French nuclear testing in the South Pacific discussed in subsection B.3.
Relevant to Aboriginal-rights issues and the Aboriginal land-claims legislation (1993) discussed in subsections B.2 and B.4.
Relevant to the indigenous peoples of Oceania and the effects of UN trusteeship administrations on them discussed in subsection A.3.

mappings.vsiTitle: The Great Depression and The New Deal
mappings.rationale: Relevant to the Great Depression's impact on Australia and New Zealand discussed in subsections B.1 and C.1.
Relevant to movements toward autonomy among indigenous peoples and the development of Australian national identity discussed throughout.
Relevant to Australia's strengthening of economic and political ties to Asian and Pacific nations and deregulation of the economy discussed in subsections B.2 and B.3.
Relevant to the UN trusteeship system in the Pacific and the dissolution of the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands discussed in subsection A.3.
